finding.contract_claim: Implicit contract: "exploration" is produced in tests/test_triage.py and checked in scripts/triage_bootstrap.py with no shared definition
finding.observed_behavior: Extract shared constants to a common module so a rename triggers an import error instead of a silent mismatch

"""V1-4 Phase C trace mining (osojicode/work#27).

Mines the *correct* exploration traces from the Phase B baseline run for the
Evidence kinds the LLM consistently consulted per finding category. The output
is the requirements doc for mechanizing the Claim Builder (see osojicode/wiki

Only ``agree: true`` rows of the baseline summary are mined — the LLM sometimes
wanders into hallucinated paths and we don't want to mechanize bad exploration.

Traces record tool-call *inputs* only. To classify a read as landing on a

referencing site, prior greps in the same trace are re-executed offline through
``ExplorationExecutor`` (read-only, root-confined) against the same roots the
exploration used. Fixture claims re-root at ``<fixture_root>/source/`` exactly
as ``triage_bootstrap.run_sdk_exploration`` did; audit claims re-execute against
the current working tree, so minor drift since the baseline commit is possible

the current working tree, so minor drift since the baseline commit is possible

finding.triage_reasoning:
Reality — the claim misidentifies `tests/test_triage.py` as the producer of 'exploration'. Examining the evidence, `scripts/triage_bootstrap.py` itself is both the origin and consumer of this string: at lines 540-541 the CLI subparser maps command name 'explore' to internal mode 'exploration', and at line 425 this mode is used in the conditional `strip_evidence=(mode == "exploration")`. The string is self-contained within triage_bootstrap.py.

The word 'exploration' in test_triage.py (line 277) is used in an English comment ('the executor output was fed back as a tool_result') and as an attribute name ('result.exploration_traces') — neither constitutes producing the string 'exploration' as a contract value.

Significance fails — there is no cross-file implicit contract. The mode string is defined, set, and consumed entirely within triage_bootstrap.py. Renaming it would only require changes within that one file's subparser and mode-check code.
